auth_notes: Requires an Integrator ID provided by Wolters Kluwer. Authentication is a multi-step process involving an initial call to the 'Authenticate' endpoint with the Integrator ID and user credentials to obtain a temporary session token (JWT), which must be included in the header of all subsequent requests.
rate_limits: Governed by the Open Integration Platform (OIP) license agreement; typically restricted to specific call volumes per minute/day depending on the firm's tier.
gotchas: Access to the API requires a paid subscription to the CCH Axcess Open Integration Platform (OIP).
The API uses OData filtering syntax which can be difficult to format correctly within n8n expressions.
Session tokens are short-lived, requiring a robust 're-authentication' logic branch within the n8n workflow.
research_notes: Integration must be handled via the HTTP Request node. A dedicated sub-workflow for authentication is recommended to handle token generation and expiration. Since webhooks are not supported, n8n must use a 'Schedule' trigger to poll the 'Get Files' or 'Get Recent Activity' endpoints to detect new uploads.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration must be handled via the HTTP Request node. A dedicated sub-workflow for authentication is recommended to handle token generation and expiration. Since webhooks are not supported, n8n must use a 'Schedule' trigger to poll the 'Get Files' or 'Get Recent Activity' endpoints to detect new uploads.
